question: Five friends A, B, C, D, and E are standing in a row facing north. A stands to the left of B. B stands to the left of C. Who stands between A and C?
explanation: The order is A - B - C. Therefore, B stands between A and C.
shortcut: Arrange people according to the given left-right clues.
learning_tip: Draw a straight line and place people from left to right.

question: Six students P, Q, R, S, T, and U are standing in a row facing north. P stands to the left of Q. Who definitely stands to the right of P?
explanation: Since P is to the left of Q, Q is definitely to the right of P.
shortcut: Use direct positional clues.
learning_tip: Not every arrangement requires building the full row.

question: Five people A, B, C, D, and E stand in a row facing north. C stands to the right of A. A stands to the right of D. Who stands to the left of both A and C?
explanation: The order is D - A - C. Therefore, D is to the left of both A and C.
shortcut: Convert all right/left clues into one sequence.
learning_tip: Link related clues before placing unrelated people.

question: Six friends M, N, O, P, Q, and R stand in a row facing north. M stands to the left of N. O stands to the left of M. P stands to the right of N. Who stands at the extreme left among O, M, N, and P?
explanation: The order is O - M - N - P. Therefore, O stands at the extreme left.
shortcut: Arrange only the required people first.
learning_tip: Ignore people not involved in the given clues.

question: Seven people A, B, C, D, E, F, and G stand in a row facing north. A stands to the left of B. B stands to the left of C. Who stands at the extreme right among A, B, and C?
explanation: The order is A - B - C. Hence, C is at the extreme right among the three.
shortcut: Create the left-to-right order.
learning_tip: Focus only on the people mentioned in the question.

question: Eight people A, B, C, D, E, F, G, and H stand in a row facing north. D stands to the left of A. A stands to the left of B. C stands to the right of B. Who stands second from the left among D, A, B, and C?
explanation: The order is D - A - B - C. Therefore, A is second from the left.
shortcut: Arrange everyone in order before counting positions.
learning_tip: Position-based questions require a complete sequence.

question: Six people P, Q, R, S, T, and U stand in a row facing north. S stands to the left of Q. Q stands to the left of P. T stands to the right of P. Who stands second from the right among S, Q, P, and T?
explanation: The order is S - Q - P - T. Therefore, P is second from the right.
shortcut: Arrange from left to right, then count from the required end.
learning_tip: Read carefully whether counting starts from the left or right.

question: Seven students A, B, C, D, E, F, and G stand in a row facing north. F stands to the left of D. D stands to the left of B. B stands to the left of A. Who stands third from the left among F, D, B, and A?
explanation: The order is F - D - B - A. Therefore, B stands third from the left.
shortcut: Write the complete order before answering position-based questions.
learning_tip: Linear arrangement questions become easy with a simple horizontal diagram.

question: Five friends A, B, C, D, and E are standing in a row facing north. B stands to the left of C. C stands to the left of D. Who stands between B and D?
explanation: The order is B - C - D. Therefore, C stands between B and D.
shortcut: Arrange the given people in order from left to right.
learning_tip: People connected by consecutive clues form a chain.

question: Six students P, Q, R, S, T, and U stand in a row facing north. R stands to the right of Q. Who definitely stands to the left of R?
explanation: Since R is to the right of Q, Q definitely stands to the left of R.
shortcut: Use direct left-right clues first.
learning_tip: Simple comparison questions do not require the full arrangement.

question: Seven people A, B, C, D, E, F, and G stand in a row facing north. D stands to the left of B. B stands to the left of A. C stands to the right of A. Who stands second from the left among D, B, A, and C?
explanation: The order is D - B - A - C. Therefore, B stands second from the left.
shortcut: Arrange the people before counting positions.
learning_tip: Always count positions only after finalizing the order.

question: Six friends M, N, O, P, Q, and R stand in a row facing north. O stands to the left of M. M stands to the left of N. P stands to the right of N. Who stands at the extreme right among O, M, N, and P?
explanation: The order is O - M - N - P. Hence, P stands at the extreme right.
shortcut: Create the arrangement using the given clues.
learning_tip: Extreme left/right questions become easy after arranging everyone.

question: Eight people A, B, C, D, E, F, G, and H stand in a row facing north. C stands to the right of A. A stands to the right of D. Who stands at the extreme left among D, A, and C?
explanation: The order is D - A - C. Therefore, D stands at the extreme left.
shortcut: Arrange only the relevant people.
learning_tip: Ignore unrelated people if they are not required.

question: Seven people P, Q, R, S, T, U, and V stand in a row facing north. T stands to the left of R. R stands to the left of Q. Q stands to the left of P. Who stands second from the right among T, R, Q, and P?
explanation: The order is T - R - Q - P. Therefore, Q stands second from the right.
shortcut: Arrange first, then count from the right.
learning_tip: Read carefully whether the question asks from the left or right.

question: Six people A, B, C, D, E, and F stand in a row facing north. F stands to the left of D. D stands to the left of C. C stands to the left of B. Who stands third from the left among F, D, C, and B?
explanation: The order is F - D - C - B. Therefore, C stands third from the left.
shortcut: Arrange all people before counting positions.
learning_tip: Position questions are easiest after drawing a row.

question: Eight people A, B, C, D, E, F, G, and H stand in a row facing north. H stands to the right of G. G stands to the right of F. F stands to the right of E. Who stands second from the left among E, F, G, and H?
explanation: The order is E - F - G - H. Therefore, F stands second from the left.
shortcut: Arrange the sequence before identifying positions.
learning_tip: Draw arrows to represent left and right relationships.

question: Five friends A, B, C, D, and E are standing in a row facing north. C stands to the right of B. B stands to the right of A. Who stands between A and C?
explanation: The order is A - B - C. Therefore, B stands between A and C.
shortcut: Write the arrangement from left to right.
learning_tip: Simple left-right clues often form a direct chain.

question: Six students P, Q, R, S, T, and U stand in a row facing north. Q stands to the left of R. Which person definitely stands to the right of Q?
explanation: Since Q is to the left of R, R is definitely to the right of Q.
shortcut: Use the direct positional clue.
learning_tip: Direct comparisons can often answer the question immediately.

question: Seven people A, B, C, D, E, F, and G stand in a row facing north. E stands to the left of C. C stands to the left of B. B stands to the left of A. Who stands second from the right among E, C, B, and A?
explanation: The order is E - C - B - A. Therefore, B stands second from the right.
shortcut: Arrange the people, then count from the required side.
learning_tip: Always pay attention to whether counting starts from the left or right.

question: Six friends M, N, O, P, Q, and R stand in a row facing north. N stands to the right of M. O stands to the left of M. P stands to the right of N. Who stands second from the left among O, M, N, and P?
explanation: The order is O - M - N - P. Hence, M stands second from the left.
shortcut: Arrange everyone before counting positions.
learning_tip: Drawing a row avoids counting mistakes.

question: Eight people A, B, C, D, E, F, G, and H stand in a row facing north. D stands to the left of B. B stands to the left of A. E stands to the right of A. Who stands at the extreme left among D, B, A, and E?
explanation: The order is D - B - A - E. Therefore, D stands at the extreme left.
shortcut: Arrange only the people mentioned in the clues.
learning_tip: Ignore unrelated people unless they affect the arrangement.

question: Seven people P, Q, R, S, T, U, and V stand in a row facing north. V stands at the extreme right. T stands to the left of R. R stands to the left of Q. Q stands to the left of P. Who stands third from the right among V, P, Q, R, and T?
explanation: The order is T - R - Q - P - V. Therefore, Q stands third from the right.
shortcut: Build the complete sequence before counting.
learning_tip: Extreme positions help anchor the arrangement.

question: Six people A, B, C, D, E, and F stand in a row facing north. E stands to the left of D. D stands to the left of C. C stands to the left of B. Who stands second from the left among E, D, C, and B?
explanation: The order is E - D - C - B. Therefore, D stands second from the left.
shortcut: Arrange from left to right before counting.
learning_tip: Count positions only after confirming the final order.

question: Eight people A, B, C, D, E, F, G, and H stand in a row facing north. H stands to the right of G. G stands to the right of F. F stands to the right of E. Who stands third from the left among E, F, G, and H?
explanation: The order is E - F - G - H. Therefore, G stands third from the left.
shortcut: Write the arrangement before answering position-based questions.
learning_tip: A simple horizontal diagram reduces mistakes.

question: Eight people A, B, C, D, E, F, G, and H stand in a row facing north. F stands to the left of D. D stands to the left of B. B stands to the left of A. G stands to the right of A. Who stands second from the right among F, D, B, A, and G?
explanation: The order is F - D - B - A - G. Therefore, A stands second from the right.
shortcut: Arrange everyone from left to right, then count from the right.
learning_tip: For linear arrangement puzzles, drawing the complete sequence first makes position-based questions much easier.
